title: onobrychis altissima
intro: Onobrychis altissima, commonly known as sainfoin or hedysarum, is a perennial legume plant native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its distinctive purple flowers and high yield of leguminous seeds. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Onobrychis altissima include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and support the immune system, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or dried extract, and is also used as a natural fertilizer due to its ability to fix nitrogen in the soil.
